slides.kicker: // sample template · luxury goods and jewellery
slides.lead: Demo (sample) presentation of the template. All text is placeholder (dummy) and serves only to showcase the styles.
slides.notes: Opening slide. Place the event name here, a one-line subtitle and a meta row at the bottom. Highlight one word with em for the accent colour.

slides.lead: Five roles, one shared standard of craft.
slides.notes: Team slide. Instead of photos it uses initials in a coloured circle, so it needs no images.

slides.lead: Three parts, with room for questions in between.
slides.notes: Agenda as three large tiles. Suitable at the start of a block.

slides.lead: Heritage, clientele and the craft behind each piece.
slides.notes: Divider slide between blocks. A large heading on a coloured gradient.

slides.lead: Materials, provenance and the hand of the maker matter more than ever. Value is born where scarcity meets story.
slides.footnote: // illustrative slide with an enlarged image
slides.notes: Hero slide: a large heading, a striking illustration and a short caption. Good for a key point or a chapter.

items: growth in high jewellery demand year over year
clients who return after a personal styling session
average time to craft a bespoke commission
bullets: Measure devotion, not footfall: the relationship is the asset.
Begin where heritage already gives you the right to lead.
One signature piece beats a wide range without a story.
slides.notes: KPI dashboard: three cards with an icon, a large number and a caption, supporting bullets below.

slides.lead: The client does not buy an object, they commission a memory.
bullets: To the occasion, the story and the wearer.
A single piece, sketched by hand and refined.
Stone by stone in the atelier, never rushed.
A finished heirloom, presented with ceremony.
slides.notes: Two-column slide: bullets with a strong lead word on the left, illustration on the right. Text scales to fit.

slides.lead: The same client, two routes and two very different margins.
cards: Buy what exists. Fast, but rarely unforgettable.
A signature piece, lightly adjusted. Warmer, still familiar.
Designed from nothing for one wearer. The highest devotion.
A house archive piece, reissued in limited number.
slides.notes: A 2x2 grid of cards. Good for comparison or a list of options.

slides.lead: Six building blocks of a house worthy of its name.
cards: Every stone traced from mine to setting.
Metal worked by hand in the atelier.
Cut, colour and clarity chosen with care.
Documented authenticity that travels with the piece.
Protection that gives the client peace of mind.
A lifetime of cleaning, resizing and repair.
slides.notes: A 3x2 grid with an icon at the head of each card. Great for an overview of tools or capabilities.

items: Growth steadies, the work is to deepen loyalty and craft.
Demand for rare pieces accelerates and reshapes the range.
The house redefines luxury itself, not only its objects.
slides.notes: Numbered cards with small trend charts and an illustration on the right. Ideal for three possible futures.

slides.lead: Five steps, each with a clear outcome.
slides.notes: A horizontal flow of steps with arrows. Each box has a heading and a small caption.

slides.quote: True luxury is never about the price of the object, it is about the depth of the attention behind it.
slides.by: Sample quote to showcase the style
slides.notes: Quote slide with a bold left rule. Keep the attribution small.

slides.lead: Simple, yet easy to forget under pressure.
bullets: Every commission carries the name of the artisan behind it.
The piece ships only when the craft is right.
Discretion and care outrank the sale, always.
slides.notes: A plain slide with bullets and a strong lead word. Text scales up automatically to fill the area.

slides.lead: A piece rushed to market is a story left untold.
slides.notes: Statement slide: one large sentence centred on the page. Use it for a key message.

slides.lead: What to do this week, this month and this season.
slides.notes: Second divider, opening the practical part.

slides.lead: We switch to the configurator and sketch a piece live.
slides.footnote: // placeholder slide for a demo or a break
slides.notes: Placeholder slide. A centred heading, ideal for a live demo, a break or questions.
Closing slide in the style of the title. Contact and a call to action.
